content: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) is the fourth leading cause of cancer death with 5-year survival ~12%. Poor prognosis due to: late presentation (vague symptoms, no screening test), aggressive biology (early metastasis, dense desmoplastic stroma limiting drug delivery), limited treatment options. Risk factors: smoking (doubles risk), chronic pancreatitis, diabetes (new-onset diabetes can be presenting symptom), obesity, family history (10% have germline mutations), genetic syndromes (BRCA1/2, PALB2, ATM, CDKN2A, STK11/Peutz-Jeghers, Lynch syndrome). Precursor lesions: pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia (PanIN), intraductal papillary mucinous neoplasm (IPMN), mucinous cystic neoplasm (MCN). Molecular features: KRAS mutations (>90%), TP53 (75%), CDKN2A (50%), SMAD4 (55%); homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) in 10-15% (BRCA1/2, PALB2, ATM); MSI-H rare (<1%). Tumor location: head (60-70%, earlier jaundice), body/tail (present later, often metastatic).
